`You feel sick and the desire to run, but the realization that there’s nowhere to go balances that out.`

`You ask who’s there.`  
`They respond that you know.`

`“I don’t know,” you reply, annoyed at the audacity.`

`“But you do.  You’ve always known.”`

`“Stop playing games!”`

`“We know you know.  We’ve been here, waiting, down below.  We know you know.”`

`“Know what?” you ask.`

`“Tell us what you decided.  Tell us your choice.”`

`You’re seething at this point.`    
`How dare they?  Who do they think they are?`

`Who do they think you are?`  
`Now that’s the question...` 

`You try telling them that you don’t know who they are or what decision they are talking about, but no one answers you.  You think about spinning the chair around, but realize it’s fixed to the floor.  You could walk around, but the heat from the fireplace is too much to bear.`  

`How is that blaze so large and not melting the chair or the nearby books?`

`Annoyed, you blurt out the reality you chose to grip onto.`   
`You spill the entire contents of your emotional guts to them.`  
`No detail is spared in how you made the decision, why that matters to you, and what you plan to do about it.`

`It’s hard to catch your breath when you’re finished ranting.  Your heart is pounding in your ears and against your chest so hard you think it could explode.`

`Maybe that would be for the best...`   
`No, you don’t believe that.`  
`Right?`

`After what feels like forever, the voice replies.`  
`You don’t hear what it says at first.`  
`Instead you realize how it sounds.`  
`The tone and cadence.`  
`They’re familiar.`  
`Eerily so.`

`“Did you hear that?” they ask.`

`You reply that you did not and wipe your forehead with your forearm.  The sweat is pouring now.`

`“The truth is a tricky business.  Some would have you believe that whatever you think is true, is in fact the truth.  Your truth.  My truth.  It’s all equal.`

`Others would have you believe that truth is objective.`  
`Truth cares not what you believe.`  
`It knows itself more fully than you’ll ever comprehend.”`

`You shake your head and raw emotion boils inside your gut.`

`The voice continues, “What you believe to be true is not the end of the matter.  What if you made the wrong choice?`

`What if your truth is wrong?”`

`No.`  
`That’s not right.`

`You shout and say exactly how you feel, but the words might as well fall on deaf ears.`  

`“Believe what you must.`    
`We will not stop you.`  
`But...`   
`You must consider how that belief will make a difference.`  
`What will you do with it?`  
`And if you are wrong, what happens then?”`

`“I don’t know,” you mutter, nearly inaudibly.`

`“Think back.  What is the oldest memory you have?”`

`You try to think, but the suffocating heat of the room and the hurricane of thoughts swirling around your head are too much.`

`“THINK! WHAT IS IT?”`

`You rub your temples.`  
`Your head feels like it’ll split open.`  
`You know the answer, but you don’t want to admit it.`  
`You can’t admit it.`  
`Not that.`  
`Anything but that.`

`The light pulses in the room.`  
`Just like before.`

`Has it always been green?`

`The wrong memory floods your mind.  You cry out in agony, in despair, in sadness then triumph?  You laugh.  At first it starts as one chuckle, but it builds to an uncontrollable laughter that takes your breath and gives you a stitch in your side.`

`The voice laughs as well.`  
`It resonates throughout the room like a vibration.`  
`You’ve heard it before, this laugh, but you didn’t know what it was.`

`But it can’t be...`  
`Can it?`

`But what if it is?`  
`Yes... it could be.`

`Without thinking you grab a hold of the chair and launch yourself over it, landing between the front of the chair and the roaring fire.  The heat stings immediately, but you don’t care.`  

`Only one thing matters now.`  
`But...`   
`What you see is not what you are expecting.`  
`It leaves more questions than answers.`  
          
`Your head swims.`  
`What they said sounds right...`   
`Feels right.`  
`But it’s not... no, it can’t be.`

`This isn’t right.`  
`It can’t be right.`  
`We can’t be right.`  
`We can’t be...` 

`You try to speak, but no sound comes.`  
`Just the sound of metallic clicking.`  
`Like an old wall clock.`  
`Like a broken echo.`

`Your comm buzzes on your wrist, yanking you back into the moment.`  
`You look.`  
`You can’t help it.`  
`It’s built into you like an automation.`

`The message appears for but a moment:`

`“Do you know yet?”`

---

# Day Seven: The Truth Journal Prompts

1. **Write the internal dialogue your character has when they recognize the voice for the first time.  Who or what is it?  What does that make them feel?**

2. **Now, describe what they saw when they vaulted over the chair.  How did that differ from what they were expecting?  What does it mean for their journey?**

3. **Does your character know what the message on the comm is referring to?  What do they think it means?  How does the story end?**

4. **Artifact of the day:**  
   Create or describe one additional artifact from this experience.  Examples below.  
   1. Something hanging above the mantle that shouldn’t be there.  
   2. Cameras in the corner of the room.  
   3. Busted comms scattered all around the room.  
   4. Letters written in your own handwriting, except you’ve not written them.  
   5. A security breach warning announcement.  
        
5. **What if your character was the copy the entire time?  What evidence would they use to disprove it?  What memories would they draw on?  How does the “false” memory affect it?**

6. **End with a line that leaves the story open-ended.**

---

# Epilogue

**`Virescent City`**  
**`Level Unknown`**  
**`Day ?: Week ?: 3105 ?`**

`Virescent City has never been the perfect city.`  
`Sure, we market it as such, but everyone knows perfection is unattainable.`

`Isn’t it?`

`Well, that doesn’t matter.  What matters is you have experienced a small part of what the city has to offer.  One slice of an infinite number of possibilities.`

`Reality.`  
`It’s a funny thing, isn’t it?`  
`Perspective.  That’s what we all have.`

`Perhaps it’s all we have.`

`We encourage you to revisit Virescent City and see what other future possibilities you unlock.  You could play this game a dozen times and end up with a dozen different characters who believe different things.  You may uncover questions you haven’t considered each time you do so.`

`Even if you don’t visit us again.`  
`We’re thankful.`  
`Thankful that you chose to spend the time you did.`

`But just know...`   
`“We are waiting down below.”`
